volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Iphigenia among the Taurians (Section 522 - Section 609)

sections.label: Section 522
sections.original: ἥκει , κακῶς γʼ ἐλθοῦσα τῶν ἐμῶν τινι .
sections.translation: She has ; it was an unfortunate arrival for one dear to me .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: καὶ ποῦ ʼστι ; κἀμοὶ γάρ τι προυφείλει κακόν .
sections.translation: And where is she ? She deserves an ill turn from me also .

sections.label: Section 524
sections.original: Σπάρτῃ ξυνοικεῖ τῷ πάρος ξυνευνέτῃ .
sections.translation: She lives at Sparta with her former bedfellow .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: ὦ μῖσος εἰς Ἕλληνας , οὐκ ἐμοὶ μόνῃ .
sections.translation: Creature hated by Hellas , not by me alone !

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: ἀπέλαυσα κἀγὼ δή τι τῶν κείνης γάμων .
sections.translation: I have also had some benefit from the marriage of that woman !

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: νόστος δʼ Ἀχαιῶν ἐγένεθʼ , ὡς κηρύσσεται ;
sections.translation: Have the Achaeans returned , as reported ?

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: ὡς πάνθʼ ἅπαξ με συλλαβοῦσʼ ἀνιστορεῖς .
sections.translation: How you put everything together and ask me all at once !

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: πρὶν γὰρ θανεῖν σε , τοῦδʼ ἐπαυρέσθαι θέλω .
sections.translation: Before you die , I want to profit by your answers .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἔλεγχʼ , ἐπειδὴ τοῦδʼ ἐρᾷς · λέξω δʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Question me , since you desire this ; I will tell you .

sections.label: Section 531
sections.original: Κάλχας τις ἦλθε μάντις ἐκ Τροίας πάλιν ;
sections.translation: Has a certain Calchas , a prophet , come back from Troy ?

sections.label: Section 532
sections.original: ὄλωλεν , ὡς ἦν ἐν Μυκηναίοις λόγος .
sections.translation: He is dead , as the story goes in Mycenae .

sections.label: Section 533
sections.original: ὦ πότνιʼ , ὡς εὖ . — τί γὰρ ὁ Λαέρτου γόνος ;
sections.translation: O goddess , how good that is ! What about Odysseus ?

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: οὔπω νενόστηκʼ οἶκον , ἔστι δʼ , ὡς λόγος .
sections.translation: He has not yet returned , but is alive , they say .

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: ὄλοιτο , νόστου μήποτʼ ἐς πάτραν τυχών .
sections.translation: May he die and never achieve a return to his country !

sections.label: Section 536
sections.original: μηδὲν κατεύχου · πάντα τἀκείνου νοσεῖ .
sections.translation: Do not pray against that man ; all is misery for him .

sections.label: Section 537
sections.original: Θέτιδος δʼ ὁ τῆς Νηρῇδος ἔστι παῖς ἔτι ;
sections.translation: But is the son of Thetis the Nereid still alive ?

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν · ἄλλως λέκτρʼ ἔγημʼ ἐν Αὐλίδι .
sections.translation: He is not ; his marriage at Aulis was in vain .

sections.label: Section 539
sections.original: δόλια γάρ , ὡς ἴσασιν οἱ πεπονθότες .
sections.translation: Yes , for it was a cheat , as those who experienced it know .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: τίς εἶ ποθʼ ; ὡς εὖ πυνθάνῃ τἀφʼ Ἑλλάδος .
sections.translation: Who are you ? How well you ask about Hellas !

sections.label: Section 541
sections.original: ἐκεῖθέν εἰμι · παῖς ἔτʼ οὖσʼ ἀπωλόμην .
sections.translation: I am from there ; while still a child I was lost .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.original: ὀρθῶς ποθεῖς ἄρʼ εἰδέναι τἀκεῖ , γύναι .
sections.translation: Then rightly you desire to know what has happened there , lady .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.original: τί δʼ ὁ στρατηγός , ὃν λέγουσʼ εὐδαιμονεῖν ;
sections.translation: What about the general , who is said to be happy ?

sections.label: Section 544
sections.original: τίς ; οὐ γὰρ ὅν γʼ ἐγᾦδα τῶν εὐδαιμόνων .
sections.translation: Who ? The one I knew was not happy .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: Ἀτρέως ἐλέγετο δή τις Ἀγαμέμνων ἄναξ .
sections.translation: There was said to be a certain lord , Agamemnon , son of Atreus .

sections.label: Section 546
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ἄπελθε τοῦ λόγου τούτου , γύναι .
sections.translation: I do not know ; leave this subject , lady .

sections.label: Section 547
sections.original: μὴ πρὸς θεῶν , ἀλλʼ εἴφʼ , ἵνʼ εὐφρανθῶ , ξένε .
sections.translation: No , by the gods , but tell me , stranger , to delight me .

sections.label: Section 548
sections.original: τέθνηχʼ ὁ τλήμων , πρὸς δʼ ἀπώλεσέν τινα .
sections.translation: The wretched man is dead , and in addition he destroyed another .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.original: τέθνηκε ; ποίᾳ συμφορᾷ ; τάλαινʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Dead ? By what fate ? I am unhappy !

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: τί δʼ ἐστέναξας τοῦτο ; μῶν προσῆκέ σοι ;
sections.translation: Why do you mourn for this ? It doesn ’ t concern you , does it ?

sections.label: Section 551
sections.original: τὸν ὄλβον αὐτοῦ τὸν πάροιθʼ ἀναστένω .
sections.translation: I grieve for his former prosperity .

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: δεινῶς γὰρ ἐκ γυναικὸς οἴχεται σφαγείς .
sections.translation: Yes , for he was dreadfully murdered by a woman .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.original: ὦ πανδάκρυτος ἡ κτανοῦσα ⋯ χὡ κτανών .
sections.translation: O miserable the slayer . . . and the slain !

sections.label: Section 554
sections.original: παῦσαί νυν ἤδη μηδʼ ἐρωτήσῃς πέρα .
sections.translation: Stop now , and do not ask further .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: τοσόνδε γʼ , εἰ ζῇ τοῦ ταλαιπώρου δάμαρ .
sections.translation: Only this much , if the wife of the wretched man is alive .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι · παῖς νιν ὃν ἔτεχʼ , οὗτος ὤλεσεν .
sections.translation: She is not ; she was killed by the son that she bore .

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: ὦ συνταραχθεὶς οἶκος . ὡς τί δὴ θέλων ;
sections.translation: O house thrown into confusion ! What was his intent ?

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: πατρὸς θανόντος τήνδε τιμωρούμενος .
sections.translation: To avenge on her the death of his father .

sections.label: Section 559
sections.original: φεῦ ·
sections.translation: Ah ! How well he exacted an evil justice !

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ τὰ πρὸς θεῶν εὐτυχεῖ δίκαιος ὤν .
sections.translation: Though he is just , he does not have good fortune from the gods .

sections.label: Section 561
sections.original: λείπει δʼ ἐν οἴκοις ἄλλον Ἀγαμέμνων γόνον ;
sections.translation: Did Agamemnon leave any other children in his house ?

sections.label: Section 562
sections.original: λέλοιπεν Ἠλέκτραν γε παρθένον μίαν .
sections.translation: He left one virgin daughter , Electra .

sections.label: Section 563
sections.original: τί δέ ; σφαγείσης θυγατρὸς ἔστι τις λόγος ;
sections.translation: What else ? Is there any report of the daughter who was sacrificed ?

sections.label: Section 564
sections.original: οὐδείς γε , πλὴν θανοῦσαν οὐχ ὁρᾶν φάος .
sections.translation: None , except that she is dead and does not see the light .

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: τάλαινʼ ἐκείνη χὡ κτανὼν αὐτὴν πατήρ .
sections.translation: Unhappy girl , and also the father that killed her !

sections.label: Section 566
sections.original: κακῆς γυναικὸς χάριν ἄχαριν ἀπώλετο .
sections.translation: As a thankless favor to an evil woman , she died .

sections.label: Section 567
sections.original: ὁ τοῦ θανόντος δʼ ἔστι παῖς Ἄργει πατρός ;
sections.translation: Does the dead father ’ s son live at Argos ?

sections.label: Section 568
sections.original: ἔστʼ , ἄθλιός γε , κοὐδαμοῦ καὶ πανταχοῦ .
sections.translation: He lives , the miserable one , both nowhere and everywhere .

sections.label: Section 569
sections.original: ψευδεῖς ὄνειροι , χαίρετʼ · οὐδὲν ἦτʼ ἄρα .
sections.translation: False dreams , farewell ; after all , you were nothing .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: οὐδʼ οἱ σοφοί γε δαίμονες κεκλημένοι
sections.translation: And those who are called wise divinities are not less false than winged dreams . These is much confusion , both in divine affairs and in human ; but only this is a grief to the one who was not foolish , but trusted in the words of prophets

sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: ὄλωλεν — ὡς ὄλωλε τοῖσιν εἰδόσιν .
sections.translation: and died — as he died to those that know .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τί δʼ ἡμεῖς οἵ τʼ ἐμοὶ γεννήτορες ;
sections.translation: Ah ! What about me , and my parents ? Are they alive ? Are they not ? Who can say ?

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: ἀκούσατʼ · ἐς γὰρ δή τινʼ ἥκομεν λόγον ,
sections.translation: Listen to me ; I have come to a subject which means benefit both to you , strangers ,

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: κἀμοί . τὸ δʼ εὖ μάλιστά γʼ οὕτω γίγνεται ,
sections.translation: and to me , by your efforts . A good action is especially so , if the same matter is pleasing to all . Would you , if I should save you , go to Argos and take a report of me to my friends there , and bring a tablet ,

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: ἔγραψεν αἰχμάλωτος , οὐχὶ τὴν ἐμὴν
sections.translation: which a captive wrote for me in pity ? He did not think my hand murderous , but that the victims of the goddess , who holds these things just , die under the law . For I have had no one to go back to Argos with that message , who ,

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: πέμψειε σωθεὶς τῶν ἐμῶν φίλων τινί .
sections.translation: being saved , would send my letter to one of my friends . But you — if , as it seems , you are not hostile to me , and you know Mycenae and those whom I want you to know — be rescued , and have this reward , not a shameful one , safety for the sake of this small letter .

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: οὗτος δʼ , ἐπείπερ πόλις ἀναγκάζει τάδε ,
sections.translation: But let him , since the city exacts it , be the offering to the goddess , separated from you .

sections.label: Section 597
sections.original: καλῶς ἔλεξας τἄλλα πλὴν ἕν , ὦ ξένη ·
sections.translation: Stranger , you have spoken all well but this : to sacrifice him would be a heavy grief to me . I am the pilot of these misfortunes ,

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ συμπλεῖ τῶν ἐμῶν μόχθων χάριν .
sections.translation: he sailed with me for the sake of my troubles . For it is not right for me to do you a favor and get out of danger , on condition of his death . But let it happen this way : give him the letter and he will take it to to Argos , for your well-being ;

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: ἡμᾶς δʼ ὁ χρῄζων κτεινέτω . τὰ τῶν φίλων
sections.translation: let anyone who wishes kill me . It is most shameful for anyone to save himself by hurling his friends ’ affairs into catastrophe . That man is my friend , and I wish him to live , no less than myself .

sections.label: Section 609
sections.original: ὦ λῆμʼ ἄριστον , ὡς ἀπʼ εὐγενοῦς τινος
sections.translation: O brave spirit ! How you were born from some noble
